page.title: Product Performance
page.subtitle: Real margin, products that sell, where revenue concentrates

intelligenceBanner.title_all_good_sub: Margins reliable and Cashflow alignment OK

products_without_cost.title: You can't tell if your products are profitable
products_without_cost.body: You're selling products worth {{blind_revenue_formatted}} — {{blind_revenue_pct}}% of revenue — without being able to compute the margin. You don't know whether those sales are in profit or in loss.
products_without_cost.cause: The {{count}} listed products don't have a "Product cost" configured. Set it in the anagraphic to see real margins immediately.

products_without_sku.title: {{count}} active products without SKU
products_without_sku.body: Products without an SKU code can't be automatically matched against CSV import rows and are harder to search. Doesn't break margins, but it's recommended hygiene.
products_without_sku.cause: SKU is not required at product creation, but it's needed for bulk imports.

suspicious_margins.title: Margins outside the normal range
suspicious_margins.body: {{negative_count}} products are selling at a loss (cost greater than price) and {{high_count}} have margin above 90% — likely missing some cost (packaging, labour, overhead).
suspicious_margins.cause: Open each suspect product: verify the "Product cost" includes all real items (raw materials, labour, energy, packaging).

cashflow_mismatch.title: Mismatch with Cashflow Monitor
cashflow_mismatch.body: Products revenue here ({{products_revenue_formatted}}) differs from total revenue in Cashflow Monitor ({{cashflow_revenue_formatted}}) by {{diff_amount_formatted}} ({{diff_pct}}%). The two views should align.
cashflow_mismatch.cause: {{orphan_count}} sales in the period are not linked to a specific product, so they appear in Cashflow but not here.

orphan_sales.title: {{count}} sales without linked product
orphan_sales.body: You have {{count}} sales in the period totalling {{amount_formatted}} not attributed to a specific product. They show up in Cashflow totals but are not counted in Product Performance metrics.
orphan_sales.cause: Typically CSV rows imported without SKU, or manual sales entered without picking a product from the catalog.

confirmed_orders_without_sales.title: {{missing_count}} orders with metrics to refresh
confirmed_orders_without_sales.body: You have {{missing_count}} confirmed orders whose revenue isn't included in this page's totals yet.
confirmed_orders_without_sales.cause: Happens when an order is confirmed without an automatic metrics update. Click "Refresh metrics now" and their revenue will appear in the totals.

purchases_unattributed.title: {{unattr_pct}}% of purchases not linked to products
purchases_unattributed.body: You have {{unattr_amount_formatted}} of purchases in the period that are not tied to a specific product. This means costs are not directly attributed and can only be estimated.
purchases_unattributed.cause: To attribute costs correctly, configure products with "From purchased item" components referencing the categories below.

unlinked_purchase_categories.title: {{count}} purchase categories not linked to products
unlinked_purchase_categories.body: There are purchase categories that aren't used in any product's cost configuration. Categories that are typically overhead (rent, utilities, software, etc.) are excluded.
unlinked_purchase_categories.cause: To improve margin precision, consider linking the most relevant categories to the products that consume them (e.g. ingredients → dish).

coverage_drop.title: Cost coverage dropping
coverage_drop.body: The share of products with cost configured dropped from {{previous_pct}}% to {{current_pct}}% vs the previous period ({{drop_pp}} percentage points less).
coverage_drop.cause: Likely you've added new products without configuring cost, or removed existing configurations.

margin_deterioration.title: Margin deteriorating
margin_deterioration.body: Weighted margin dropped from {{previous_pct}}% to {{current_pct}}% vs the previous period ({{drop_pp}} percentage points less).
margin_deterioration.cause: Possible causes: rising purchase costs, more aggressive discounts, sales mix shifted toward lower-margin products.

empty.title: No product data yet
empty.description: Data will appear once you upload sales with an SKU column matching your products, or once a confirmed storefront order generates sales records.

banners.no_cost_data_body: You haven't configured the cost of your products yet. Open the product anagraphic and use the "Product cost" section to set the unit cost or link items from Purchases.
banners.partial_coverage_body: You're computing margin for {{pct}}% of your products. Configure the cost of the remaining ones for complete analysis.

totalRevenue.title: Product revenue
totalRevenue.def: Sum of revenue generated by sold products in the selected period. Excludes any revenue not tied to a specific product.
totalRevenue.calc: Σ sales × unit price (across all products, in the period).
totalRevenue.read: This is the sales volume of your products, not the total business turnover. If the figure seems low, check that your sales records have the SKU linked to a product.

totalCost.title: Product cost (COGS)
totalCost.def: Total cost of sold products. Based on the "Product cost" configured in the product anagraphic (manual or computed from purchases).
totalCost.calc: Σ (unit cost × quantity sold) for each product with a configured cost.
totalCost.read: The "true" cost of goods sold. If N/A, you haven't configured costs yet — open the product anagraphic to set them.

weightedMargin.title: Weighted margin
weightedMargin.def: The overall margin % of your products, weighted by each product's revenue. Reflects the real business margin.
weightedMargin.read: Above 30%: excellent for most sectors. 10%–30%: in the norm. Below 10%: products may not cover indirect costs — consider revising prices or costs.

avgMargin.title: Average margin per product
avgMargin.def: Simple mean of your products' margin %. Each product weighs equally regardless of sales volume.
avgMargin.calc: (Σ margin % per product) ÷ number of products with data
avgMargin.read: If significantly different from Weighted margin, you have a heterogeneous product mix. Compare with the weighted margin to see if your "stars" outperform the average or not.

activeProducts.title: Active products
activeProducts.def: Number of published or available products in the catalog. Does not consider sales generated.
activeProducts.calc: Count of products with is_active = true at read time.
activeProducts.read: The "perimeter" of your catalog. Compare with "Products with sales" below to see how much of the catalog is actually working.

productsWithSales.title: Products with sales
productsWithSales.def: Number of products that generated at least one sale in the selected period.
productsWithSales.calc: Count of products with ≥1 sale record in the period.
productsWithSales.read: If much lower than "Active products", you have dead-stock: products occupying catalog space without producing revenue. Consider suspending or promoting the inactive ones.

topSeller.title: Top seller
topSeller.def: The product that generated the most revenue in the selected period.
topSeller.calc: argmax(revenue per product) in the period.
topSeller.read: Your locomotive product. Knowing the top seller helps decide where to invest in marketing, stock and operational attention.

mostProfitable.title: Most profitable
mostProfitable.def: The product that generated the highest absolute profit (revenue minus cost) in the selected period.
mostProfitable.calc: argmax(revenue − cost) per product with configured cost.
mostProfitable.read: Not necessarily the top seller: a product can sell less but with a margin so high it generates more profit. These are your "profitability champions".

costCoverage.title: Cost data coverage
costCoverage.def: Percentage of products with sales for which you have configured a "Product cost" in the anagraphic.
costCoverage.calc: (Products with cost configured ÷ Products with sales) × 100
costCoverage.read: 100%: margins are 100% reliable. Below 70%: part of the margins is estimated or missing. For complete analysis, finish configuring costs on the remaining products.

top10Concentration.title: Top 10 concentration
top10Concentration.def: How much of your revenue comes from the 10 best-selling products.
top10Concentration.calc: (Σ revenue of top 10 products ÷ Total revenue) × 100
top10Concentration.read: Above 80%: strong dependency on a few top — if one of them dips, revenue suffers. Below 40%: well-distributed portfolio, more resilient but with less commercial leverage.

abc.title: ABC distribution
abc.def: Classifies products by their contribution to revenue. An application of the Pareto principle (80/20) to your catalog.
abc.calc: Sort products by descending revenue, then: A = top that cumulate 80% of revenue; B = next ones up to 95%; C = the remaining 5%.
abc.read: Class A: products to focus attention, stock and marketing on. Class B: useful but not critical products. Class C: candidates to reduce or eliminate unless they have strategic reasons to stay.

categories.title: Top categories
categories.def: The product categories that generate the most revenue in the period.
categories.calc: Aggregated by Product.category, sorted by descending revenue.
categories.read: Shows where the business concentrates by product family. Useful for assortment and marketing decisions.

table.name: Product
table.no_data_desc: Data will appear once you upload sales with the SKU column linked to products created in Master data.
table.margin_unavailable: Margins are not yet available. To compute per-product margins, configure the "Product cost" in the anagraphic of your products.

profile.title: Product detail
profile.cost_breakdown_empty: Product cost not yet configured. Open it in the anagraphic to set it.

cost.partial_coverage: Margins are computed only for {{pct}}% of products. Configure the cost of the remaining products for complete analysis.
